ton. The letter received today is
the promised letter, written from
Butte, October 5, to Mr.. Levey and
by him transmitted to the mayor.


Mr.. Elliott begins by referring to
the request contained in a letter
from w. B. Roberts, chairman of
the committee of IS business men
of Spokane, which letter was pre-
scented by Mayor Daggett. He says
he can not comply with the COM
mittee's request to lower his tracks
through the city nor to use the
Great Northern depot. Concerning
the latter proposition, he states the
Great Northern is none too large
for one road and would not be able
to accommodate two.


He says he has three propositions
to make.


First: He will pay the expenses
of a committee of five to visit east
ern cities and inspect subways,
methods of construction, feasibility.
sightliness and safety. He wants
the committee to report on the pro-
portions of expense borne by the
